avalancheActivity.highlights: Increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
From late morning the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase. As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation wet loose snow avalanches are possible, especially in case of releases originating from very steep starting zones that still retain some snow.

As the penetration by moisture increases wet slab avalanches are possible as the day progresses, in particular medium-sized ones. This applies especially in case of releases originating from shady starting zones, but in isolated cases also in case of releases originating from high-altitude sunny starting zones that have retained the snow thus far.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Up to 2600 m rain will fall from midday in some regions. Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good at times. The surface of the snowpack will soften quickly. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to rapid and thorough wetting of the snowpack below approximately 3000 m.
West, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: In some regions up to 10 cm of snow will fall. This applies in particular at elevated altitudes in the Venediger Range and in the Glockner Range.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches.
From origins in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place natural avalanches are possible, in particular medium-sized ones. This applies on shady slopes below approximately 2900 m, as well as on sunny slopes at elevated altitudes. On steep shady slopes avalanches can be released in the weakly bonded old snow and reach large size in isolated cases.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2400 m. Such avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.

Backcountry tours and off-piste skiing should be started and concluded early.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Some rain will fall from midday in some regions. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will already soften in the late morning. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack below approximately 2900 m. The high humditiy will give rise in the afternoon to a loss of strength within the snowpack also on steep shady slopes.

Steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2400 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.

Below approximately 1800 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.

tendency.highlights: Wet snow requires caution. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Some rain will fall in some regions.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The conditions in the morning are spring-like. The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust. The weather conditions from the late morning will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack.

In starting zones where no previous releases have taken place and on very steep slopes more small and medium-sized gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible as the penetration by moisture increases.
High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: The near-surface layers of the snowpack can be released in isolated cases by individual winter sport participants.

From late morning the weather will be partly cloudy. Backcountry tours should be started early and concluded timely.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Below approximately 3000 m: The snowpack is moist and its surface has a melt-freeze crust. The surface of the snowpack will already soften in the late morning.

As a consequence of mild temperatures and sleet the snowpack will settle at the weekend. This applies in particular at low and intermediate altitudes.

The high humditiy will give rise to moistening of the snowpack. This applies in particular on sunny slopes at intermediate and high altitudes, and in all aspects at low and intermediate altitudes.
Below approximately 2400 m only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes.

tendency.highlights: Monday: Little snow will fall in some localities. The avalanche danger will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. In localities where a lot of rain falls the avalanche danger is greater. Wet avalanches can in some places be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies on very steep shady slopes below approximately 2500 m, as well as on very steep sunny slopes at elevated altitudes. Small and medium-sized natural avalanches are possible in the afternoon.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Up to 2600 m rain will fall from midday in some regions. Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good at times. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day. The spring-like weather conditions will give rise to rapid softening of the snowpack at intermediate and high altitudes.
Steep slopes: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Some rain will fall in some localities. The danger of wet avalanches will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will already soften in the late morning.
In particular on very steep slopes and in steep rocky terrain more small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist snow slides and avalanches are possible as the penetration by moisture increases. During the night as well, individual gliding avalanches are possible.

High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: The near-surface layers of the snowpack can be released in isolated cases by individual winter sport participants.
Naturally triggered avalanches and penetration of the old snow cover show the existence of a weak snowack.

Backcountry tours should be concluded timely.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The snowpack is moist and its surface has a melt-freeze crust.

The spring-like weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack.
Below approximately 2400 m only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes. Above approximately 1600 m there is still a very large amount of snow on north facing slopes.

tendency.highlights: Monday: Little rain will fall in some localities. The avalanche danger will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The weather will be partly cloudy over a wide area.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on steep shady slopes above approximately 2800 m.
As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes below approximately 2800 m.
Medium-sized avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day.
As a consequence of wind, mostly shallow wind slabs formed in high Alpine regions.
Steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.
Below approximately 2100 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.

region.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase quickly during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Early morning: The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze. Where cloud cover will be heaviest during the night.
From late morning the natural activity of avalanches will increase.
In particular in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place and on very steep slopes more medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large natural wet avalanches are possible.
The conditions are generally favourable for ski touring. Backcountry tours and ascents to alpine cabins should be concluded timely.

In the course of the day mostly small wind slabs will form especially above approximately 3000 m.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The wind will be moderate in some localities.
The weather conditions gave rise to rapid settling of the snowpack.
At higher altitudes, snow cover becomes thinner and patchy; it is better at mid-altitudes, which are less exposed to the wind. Below approximately 2000 m hardly any snow is lying.
The weather conditions gave rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2500 m.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights:
More moist and wet avalanches are possible.
As a consequence of the moderate northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding snow require caution. From starting zones where no previous releases have taken place gliding avalanches can reach intermediate altitudes.
avalancheActivity.comment: In all aspects more medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large gliding avalanches and wet snow slides are possible especially in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place. Great caution and restraint are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment: As a consequence of mild temperatures and rain up to high altitudes an unfavourable avalanche situation developed during the last two days. The snowpack is wet. Gliding avalanches can be released at any time of day or night.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced in some case. More moist snow slides and avalanches are possible.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In particular in the Prealps a partly overcast night. The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze.
From late morning the weather will be partly cloudy. The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already increase in the late morning.

In particular on very steep slopes more small and medium-sized moist and wet avalanches are possible as the moisture increases.

Backcountry tours should be started very early and concluded timely.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night was reduced in some cases.
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will soften earlier than the day before. This applies in particular below the tree line.

Below approximately 2200 m no snow is lying on south facing slopes.

Some rain will fall in particular in the Prealps. As a consequence of mild temperatures and high relative humidity the snowpack can not consolidate during the next few days.

tendency.highlights: Monday: Little rain will fall. The avalanche danger will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Over a wide area rain. As the moisture increases the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent.
avalancheActivity.comment: Wet and gliding avalanches are possible. The avalanche danger should be investigated very thoroughly in the relevant locality. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Places where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially precarious. The avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack.

snowpackStructure.comment: Snow cover and thickness vary greatly depending on exposure and altitude, and are steadily decreasing. Weather conditions will further soak the snowpack in many areas. Over a wide area a little snow is lying on south facing slopes. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be cloudy. Over a wide area rain below approximately 2000 m.

avalancheActivity.highlights: In some regions up to 5 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall above approximately 2700 m.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche prone locations for moist avalanches are to be found in particular on cut slopes below approximately 2800 m. Bases of rock walls are especially unfavourable. From starting zones that have retained some snow on very steep shady slopes moist avalanches can in very isolated cases reach large size. The more recent wind slabs are to be found especially in the vicinity of peaks. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls in northwest to north to northeast facing aspects above approximately 2500 m. Transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially dangerous.
Hardly any increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as a consequence of the precipitation.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Precarious weak layers exist in the snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack in some places in particular on steep slopes below approximately 2700 m. The new snow will be deposited on a weakly bonded old snowpack below approximately 2700 m.

avalancheActivity.comment: Only isolated wet avalanches are possible. Caution is to be exercised on extremely steep shady slopes. Mostly avalanches are rather small.

snowpackStructure.comment: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.

tendency.highlights: Low avalanche danger will prevail. Wet snow requires caution.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The weather will be partly cloudy over a wide area.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will soften during the day.
In isolated cases the avalanches are small.

snowpackStructure.comment: As a consequence of mild temperatures the snowpack consolidated. As a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches.

avalancheActivity.comment:
Only isolated wet avalanches are possible. Caution is to be exercised on extremely steep shady slopes. Mostly avalanches are rather small.

Below approximately 2000 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.

snowpackStructure.comment: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.

tendency.highlights: Low avalanche danger will prevail. Wet snow requires caution.
